import java.util.*

fun solve(n: Long, edges: Array<Array<Long>>): Long {
    val nInt = n.toInt()
    val outgoingEdges = Array(nInt) { mutableListOf<Pair<Long, Long>>() }
    val incomingEdges = Array(nInt) { mutableListOf<Pair<Long, Long>>() }

    for (edge in edges) {
        val u = edge[0]
        val v = edge[1]
        val w = edge[2]
        outgoingEdges[u.toInt()].add(Pair(v, w))
        incomingEdges[v.toInt()].add(Pair(u, w))
    }

    val dist = Array(nInt) { IntArray(2) { Long.MAX_VALUE } }
    dist[0][0] = 0L

    val pq = PriorityQueue<Triple<Long, Long, Long>>(compareBy { it.first })
    pq.add(Triple(0L, 0L, 0L))

    while (pq.isNotEmpty()) {
        val (cost, u, s) = pq.poll()
        if (cost > dist[u.toInt()][s.toInt()]) continue

        // Process outgoing edges
        for (edge in outgoingEdges[u.toInt()]) {
            val (v, w) = edge
            val newCost = cost + w
            if (newCost < dist[v.toInt()][0]) {
                dist[v.toInt()][0] = newCost
                pq.add(Triple(newCost, v, 0))
            }
        }

        // If switch not used, process incoming edges
        if (s == 0) {
            for (edge in incomingEdges[u.toInt()]) {
                val (v, w) = edge
                val newCost = cost + 2 * w
                if (newCost < dist[v.toInt()][0]) {
                    dist[v.toInt()][0] = newCost
                    pq.add(Triple(newCost, v, 0))
                }
            }
        }
    }

    val result = minOf(dist[nInt - 1][0], dist[nInt - 1][1])
    return if (result == Long.MAX_VALUE) -1 else result
}
